deck.name: Entertainment & Pop Culture
deck.description: Vocabulary for movies, music, TV shows, streaming, and modern trends.

cards.definition: to watch multiple episodes of a TV show in one sitting
cards.context: I binge-watched the entire season of that new show last weekend.

cards.definition: a dramatic and exciting ending to an episode that leaves the audience in suspense
cards.context: The season finale ended on a massive cliffhanger.

cards.definition: information that reveals important plot points and ruins the surprise
cards.context: Don't tell me the ending! No spoilers, please!

cards.definition: short for 'independent'; produced by smaller, non-mainstream companies
cards.context: I really enjoy discovering new indie bands on Bandcamp.

cards.definition: a song or album that reaches the number one spot on the music charts
cards.context: Their latest single is already a chart-topper in ten countries.

cards.definition: a very popular and successful movie or book
cards.context: The summer is always full of big-budget Hollywood blockbusters.

cards.definition: a movie or show that has a small but very dedicated following
cards.context: 'The Rocky Horror Picture Show' is the ultimate cult classic.

cards.definition: combining elements from different styles or categories
cards.context: This artist's music is genre-bending, mixing jazz with heavy metal.

cards.definition: a radical change in the expected direction or outcome of a story
cards.context: I never saw that plot twist coming; I was totally shocked!

cards.definition: the community of fans of a particular person, team, or franchise
cards.context: The Marvel fandom is incredibly active on social media.

cards.definition: a brief appearance by a famous person in a movie or show
cards.context: Did you spot the director's cameo in the second scene?

cards.definition: a new show or movie derived from an existing one
cards.context: Better Call Saul is a highly successful spin-off of Breaking Bad.

cards.definition: a new version of an older movie or show
cards.context: I usually prefer the original version over the modern remake.

cards.definition: to start a franchise over from the beginning with a new direction
cards.context: The Spider-Man franchise has been rebooted several times.

cards.definition: becoming very popular very quickly via the internet
cards.context: Her funny video went viral and got millions of views overnight.

cards.definition: an image, video, or piece of text that is spread rapidly by internet users
cards.context: That cat meme is everywhere on my feed right now.

cards.definition: a person with the ability to influence potential buyers via social media
cards.context: Many brands partner with influencers to reach younger audiences.

cards.definition: an online platform for watching videos or listening to music (e.g., Netflix, Spotify)
cards.context: I have too many subscriptions to different streaming services.

cards.definition: the music used in a movie or show
cards.context: The soundtrack for Interstellar is absolutely beautiful.

cards.definition: having received one or more prizes for excellence
cards.context: He is an award-winning actor known for his dramatic roles.

cards.definition: receiving very positive reviews from professional critics
cards.context: The play is critically acclaimed but not very popular with the general public.

cards.definition: referring to art or music that is outside the mainstream
cards.context: The city has a thriving underground techno scene.

cards.definition: the ideas, attitudes, or activities that are shared by most people
cards.context: Pop music is the most mainstream genre in the world.

cards.definition: a live performance by a musician or band
cards.context: Are you going to the gig tonight?

cards.definition: the place where an event or performance happens
cards.context: The concert venue was an old converted warehouse.

cards.definition: the area behind the stage in a theater or concert hall
cards.context: We were lucky enough to get backstage passes to meet the band.

cards.definition: used to refer to the commercial success of a movie in terms of ticket sales
cards.context: The movie was a huge hit at the box office.

cards.definition: an artist known for only one successful song
cards.context: Many famous artists from the 80s were actually one-hit wonders.

cards.definition: intensive promotion or publicity; exaggerated excitement
cards.context: There was so much hype around the release of the new video game.

cards.definition: currently popular or widely discussed on social media
cards.context: What's trending on Twitter right now?

cards.definition: a strip of red carpet laid on the ground for high-ranking visitors or celebrities
cards.context: All eyes were on the celebrities as they walked the red carpet at the Oscars.

cards.definition: freelance photographers who pursue celebrities to get candid shots
cards.context: The actress was constantly followed by the paparazzi.

cards.definition: referring to the most famous and successful celebrities
cards.context: The party was full of A-list actors and musicians.

cards.definition: a very popular and successful movie or book (already present, adding more detail)
cards.context: The new superhero movie is expected to be a summer blockbuster.

cards.definition: the art of making motion pictures
cards.context: The cinematography in that film was absolutely stunning.

cards.definition: the main character in a story, movie, or play
cards.context: The protagonist of the novel is a young detective.

cards.definition: a person who actively opposes or is hostile to someone or something; an adversary
cards.context: The Joker is the primary antagonist of Batman.

cards.definition: the script of a movie, including acting instructions and scene directions
cards.context: She won an award for best original screenplay.

cards.definition: a short extract from a movie or program used for promotion
cards.context: The trailer for the new sci-fi movie looks amazing.

cards.definition: a period of prolonged applause during which the audience rises to its feet
cards.context: The performance was so good that the cast received a standing ovation.

cards.definition: a repeated or additional performance of an item at the end of a concert
cards.context: The band came back for two encores because the crowd wouldn't stop cheering.

cards.definition: the place where tickets are sold; used to measure a movie's commercial success
cards.context: The movie was a huge hit at the box office.

cards.definition: referring to films or music produced by small, independent companies
cards.context: I really enjoy watching indie films at the local cinema.

cards.definition: a warning that an important plot point is about to be revealed
cards.context: Spoiler alert! If you haven't seen the finale, don't read this article.

cards.definition: referring to a TV show that is very addictive and easy to watch in one go
cards.context: The new thriller series is definitely binge-worthy.
